message: br-bd-3fjz: pin fsopen null EPERM parity

 {"id":"bd-3f6f","title":"CI: comprehensive pipeline integration","description":"Comprehensive CI pipeline for all quality gates.

Goal: Automated CI that validates all FrankenLibC quality gates.

CI Jobs:

1. Build Matrix
   - cargo build --release (all targets)
   - cargo build --release -p frankenlibc-abi
   - cargo build --features=standalone (L2+)

2. Quality Gates
   - cargo fmt --check
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
   - cargo test --all-targets

3. Conformance Suite
   - scripts/ci.sh (basic gate)
   - scripts/conformance_golden_gate.sh
   - scripts/snapshot_gate.sh

4. LD_PRELOAD Smoke
   - scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh (strict)
   - FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh

5. Performance Gates
   - cargo bench -p frankenlibc-bench
   - Strict <20ns validation
   - Hardened <200ns validation
   - No regressions vs baseline

6. CVE Arena
   - All CVE triggers pass in hardened mode
   - Metrics capture for evidence

7. Fuzzing (nightly)
   - 1M iterations per fuzz target
   - Crash reporting
   - Coverage report

8. Multi-arch (when available)
   - aarch64 cross-compile
   - QEMU test run

Artifacts:
- Build logs
- Test results (JUnit XML)
- Coverage reports
- Benchmark results
- Fuzzing coverage

Success Criteria:
- All gates pass on every PR
- Performance baselines maintained
- Coverage reports generated
- Nightly fuzzing runs

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmBadger","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:05.833424316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:10.377640263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:10.377621768Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive CI pipeline integration: core/extended/release/fuzz/multi-arch lanes, explicit fuzz execution budgets, artifact persistence, and validated command viability with rch offload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":972,"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16): started `bd-3f6f` and landed CI workflow bootstrap.

Implemented first integration slice:
- Added `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (new CI pipeline wiring).

What it covers:
1) Core lane (`core-gates`):
- Triggered on PRs and pushes.
- Installs nightly + rustfmt/clippy.
- Runs `scripts/ci.sh`.
- Uploads CI logs artifact (`core-ci-artifacts`).

2) Extended lane (`extended-gates`):
- Runs on schedule, `main`, or manual dispatch with `run_extended=true`.
- Runs `FRANKENLIBC_EXTENDED_GATES=1 scripts/ci.sh`.
- Runs `TIMEOUT_SECONDS=10 scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh`.
- Uploads logs and generated gate outputs from `target/*` (`extended-ci-artifacts`).

3) Operational hardening:
- Added workflow `concurrency` with cancel-in-progress.
- Added retention windows and `if: always()` artifact upload for failure triage.

Notes:
- This is the first scoped slice for `bd-3f6f` (pipeline wiring baseline). Next slices can expand with explicit release/fuzz/nightly matrix lanes and additional artifact indexing contracts.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:30:01Z"},{"id":973,"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16): completed second CI integration slice in .github/workflows/ci.yml.\n\nAdditions in this slice:\n1) Build matrix/release lane hardening\n- release workspace build now uses: cargo build --workspace --all-targets --release\n- keeps ABI-specific release build and conditional standalone feature build\n\n2) Fuzz nightly execution lane\n- kept architecture/readiness gates:\n  - scripts/check_fuzz_harness_architecture.sh\n  - scripts/check_fuzz_phase1_targets.sh\n  - scripts/check_fuzz_membrane_validation.sh (new in lane)\n- added per-target nightly run budget execution:\n  - cargo fuzz run fuzz_string -- -runs=1000000\n  - cargo fuzz run fuzz_malloc -- -runs=1000000\n  - cargo fuzz run fuzz_membrane -- -runs=1000000\n  - cargo fuzz run fuzz_printf -- -runs=1000000\n- added deterministic summary artifact generation:\n  - artifacts/ci/fuzz-summary.v1.json\n- expanded uploaded artifacts to include fuzz run logs, cargo-fuzz crash artifacts, and corpus snapshots\n\n3) Multi-arch readiness lane\n- if cross linker is available:\n  - cargo check --workspace --all-targets --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu\n  - cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu --release\n- added best-effort qemu probe execution + logs when qemu-aarch64 is available\n\nValidation performed:\n- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml"); puts "yaml-ok"'\n- Result: yaml-ok\n\nStatus:\n- Bead remains in progress while I perform an additional pass against acceptance criteria/evidence expectations before closure recommendation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:35:59Z"},{"id":974,"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional validation + unblock fix (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16):\n\nWhile validating the new fuzz lane commands, I found and fixed a real blocker that would have broken CI:\n\nIssue discovered:\n-  failed against  with workspace-manifest resolution errors unless  is provided and the fuzz crate declares an explicit local workspace boundary.\n\nFixes applied:\n1) \n- Added an explicit empty  table to prevent cargo-fuzz workspace confusion under the monorepo layout.\n\n2) \n- Updated fuzz commands to use explicit fuzz dir:\n  - fuzz_malloc
fuzz_membrane
fuzz_printf
fuzz_string\n  - \n\nValidation evidence:\n- YAML parse: PASS\n  - yaml-ok\n- Fuzz target discovery command: PASS\n  - fuzz_malloc
fuzz_membrane
fuzz_printf
fuzz_string\n  - targets: fuzz_malloc, fuzz_membrane, fuzz_printf, fuzz_string\n- Fuzz execution smoke via required offload (): PASS\n  - \n  - completed successfully with \n\nNotes:\n- Running fuzz readiness gates locally also passed and refreshed the deterministic fuzz report artifacts under  (timestamp/content updates now reflected in working tree).\n- Bead remains  pending final closure pass against full acceptance evidence checklist.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:38:13Z"},{"id":975,"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTED UPDATE (supersedes prior malformed shell-expanded line noise):

Additional validation + unblock fix (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16):

Issue discovered:
- cargo fuzz run failed against crates/frankenlibc-fuzz due workspace-manifest resolution under monorepo layout unless explicit fuzz-dir/workspace boundaries are provided.

Fixes applied:
1) crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml
- Added explicit empty [workspace] table.

2) .github/workflows/ci.yml
- Updated fuzz commands to use explicit fuzz dir:
  - cargo fuzz list --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz
  - cargo fuzz run --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz <target> -- -runs=...

Validation evidence:
- YAML parse: PASS
  command: ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml"); puts "yaml-ok"'
  result: yaml-ok

- Fuzz target discovery: PASS
  command: cargo fuzz list --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz
  result targets: fuzz_malloc, fuzz_membrane, fuzz_printf, fuzz_string

- Fuzz execution smoke via required offload: PASS
  command: rch exec -- cargo fuzz run --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz fuzz_membrane -- -runs=64 -max_total_time=10
  result: Done 64 runs

- Fuzz readiness gates: PASS
  commands:
  scripts/check_fuzz_harness_architecture.sh
  scripts/check_fuzz_phase1_targets.sh
  scripts/check_fuzz_membrane_validation.sh

Notes:
- Running fuzz readiness gates refreshed deterministic fuzz report artifacts in tests/conformance.
- bd-3f6f remains in_progress pending final closure checklist review.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:38:32Z"},{"id":976,"issue_id":"bd-3f6f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closure-evidence checklist draft (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16):\n\nImplemented CI job coverage vs bead scope:\n- Build matrix: release workspace all-targets + ABI release + standalone-feature build path (conditional).\n- Quality gates: scripts/ci.sh now runs cargo fmt --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --all-targets.\n- Conformance/snapshot/perf/CVE: covered through FRANKENLIBC_EXTENDED_GATES=1 scripts/ci.sh in extended lane.\n- LD_PRELOAD strict+hardened: extended lane runs scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh (script executes both modes).\n- Fuzzing: nightly/manual-extended lane runs readiness gates + per-target cargo-fuzz run budget (1,000,000) + uploads crash/corpus artifacts + deterministic summary json.\n- Multi-arch: aarch64 workspace check and ABI cross-build when linker exists; qemu-aarch64 probe run when available.\n\nVerification commands run locally:\n1) YAML validation\n   ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml"); puts "yaml-ok"'\n   Expected: yaml-ok\n\n2) Fuzz target discovery\n   cargo fuzz list --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz\n   Expected targets: fuzz_malloc, fuzz_membrane, fuzz_printf, fuzz_string\n\n3) Fuzz command viability (offloaded)\n   rch exec -- cargo fuzz run --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz fuzz_membrane -- -runs=64 -max_total_time=10\n   Expected terminal marker: Done 64 runs\n\n4) Quality-gate all-target compile viability (offloaded)\n   rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-run\n   Expected: Finished  profile + executable inventory output\n\nRepresentative failure signatures for triage:\n- YAML invalid: parser exception from ruby YAML.load_file\n- Fuzz dir miswire: could not read manifest file .../fuzz/Cargo.toml\n- Fuzz workspace mismatch: current package believes it's in a workspace when it's not\n- Fuzz crash indicator: libFuzzer lines containing 'deadly signal' or generated crash artifact paths\n\nThis establishes closure evidence for bd-3f6f implementation; if no objections I will close the bead and release reservations.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3fb","title":"EPIC: Extreme Optimization Loop (baselines + profiles + goldens)","description":"Enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop for allocator + membrane:
BASELINE -> PROFILE -> PROVE -> IMPLEMENT (one lever) -> VERIFY -> REPEAT.

Deliverables:
- Baseline suite (hyperfine + criterion).
- Flamegraph/profiler playbook.
- Golden outputs + isomorphism proof template wired into PR process.

Acceptance:
- We can prove behavior unchanged after optimizations via goldens/checksums.
- Perf regressions are detected quickly and reproducibly.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:07.189454921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:02.023072672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:02.023028389Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-1ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-22p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-26o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2bd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2r0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-2wp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3fb","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3fil","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_fault reusable fault injection","description":"Integrate with franken_fault for reusable fault injection: concurrency (cancellation at worst points, ABA hazards, starvation), time (virtual time drift), memory (OOM, corruption injection). Must support FrankenLab scenario runner: frankenlab run <scenario> with declarative YAML scenarios.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.281414666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:40:34.245391631Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:40:34.245201431Z","close_reason":"Integrated reusable fault-injection artifacts in crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/fault_injection.rs, extended fault_injection_framework_test coverage, and verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test fault_injection_framework_test plus workspace cargo check/fmt.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-crate","fault-injection","frankenlibc","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fil","depends_on_id":"bd-2hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1408,"issue_id":"bd-3fil","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update 2026-04-07

Completed artifact/evidence integration for the reusable fault-injection slice in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/fault_injection.rs` and extended `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/fault_injection_framework_test.rs` to assert artifact join keys and manifest indexing.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test fault_injection_framework_test` -> PASS (3 tests)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` -> PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` -> blocked by pre-existing unrelated formatting drift in `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/elimination_backoff_artifacts_test.rs`

Notes:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness fault_injection_framework_test` only compiled and matched zero tests; reran with `--test fault_injection_framework_test` for the actual integration-test execution.
- Issue remains dependency-blocked by `bd-2hh`, so this is a completed slice/progress note rather than closure.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:18Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3fjz","title":"syscall: fsopen NULL fsname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fsopen(NULL, 0) fails with EPERM rather than a pointer-validation errno. Add focused ABI parity coverage so future wrapper changes keep that privilege-path behavior explicit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:43:54.347787574Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:43:54.347787574Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3fjz","title":"syscall: fsopen NULL fsname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fsopen(NULL, 0) fails with EPERM rather than a pointer-validation errno. Add focused ABI parity coverage so future wrapper changes keep that privilege-path behavior explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:43:54.347787574Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:05:36.355977823Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:05:36.353773355Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3g4p","title":"Support matrix: automated maintenance","description":"Automated support matrix maintenance.

Goal: Keep support_matrix.json synchronized with code reality.

Automation Components:

1. Symbol Discovery
   - Parse exported symbols from libfrankenlibc_abi.so
   - Compare against support_matrix.json
   - Report new/removed symbols

2. Status Validation
   - For Implemented: verify no todo!/unimplemented!
   - For RawSyscall: verify syscall veneer usage
   - For GlibcCallThrough: verify glibc call
   - For Stub: verify deterministic fallback

3. Conformance Linkage
   - Each symbol links to fixture files
   - Each symbol links to test commands
   - Coverage report generation

4. Drift Detection
   - CI gate fails on drift
   - PR comments with drift summary
   - Automated fix suggestions

5. Report Generation
   - reality_report.v1.json from support_matrix
   - Feature parity reports
   - Coverage dashboards

CI Integration:
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on drift
- Generate coverage reports

Success Criteria:
- Zero manual maintenance of support_matrix
- Drift detected within one PR
- Reports always current

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"2026-03-13 GoldSquirrel picked up bd-3g4p via bv robot triage. Scope this session: stop support-matrix maintenance verification from rewriting tests/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json, generate a temp report under target/conformance instead, compare stable sections against the canonical artifact, and add regression coverage that the gate does not mutate the canonical report.

Progress this session:
- Updated scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh so the gate writes to target/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance.generated.json instead of mutating the checked-in canonical report.
- Added stable-section drift comparison against tests/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json for the current-state fields only (schema_version, bead, summary, coverage_dashboard, status_distribution, module_coverage, symbol_status_map, status_validation_issues, unlinked_symbols), with an explicit note that baseline-relative transition fields are intentionally excluded from strict equality.
- Added an env override seam for the canonical maintenance report path so the failure path can be tested without touching the checked-in artifact.
- Expanded crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/support_matrix_maintenance_test.rs so maintenance report generation uses a unique target/conformance temp output, verifies stable-section parity with the canonical artifact, asserts the maintenance gate does not rewrite the canonical report on any exit path, and forces a stable-section drift failure case that checks for the new canonical_stable_sections error event.

Verification this session:
- Local: bash scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh ✅
- rch check ✅
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_goldsquirrel_test2 cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test support_matrix_maintenance_test -- --nocapture ✅ (9 passed)
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_goldsquirrel_check cargo check --workspace --all-targets ✅
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_goldsquirrel_clippy_harness2 cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --test support_matrix_maintenance_test -- -D warnings ✅
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_goldsquirrel_clippy cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings ❌ pre-existing unrelated lint in crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/property_tests.rs:455 (manual_range_contains)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check ❌ pre-existing unrelated formatting drift across many ABI and membrane files; failure is not in the touched maintenance gate files

Status: keeping bd-3g4p in progress. This session closes the no-rewrite / canonical-drift guard gap, but the broader automated maintenance bead still includes wider support-matrix/report automation scope beyond this regression fix.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldSquirrel","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:51.115197623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.982425148Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.982209620Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","matrix"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3g4p","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3g4p","depends_on_id":"bd-3f6f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3h1u","title":"EPIC: Methodology and Infrastructure","description":"Infrastructure and methodology beads that support the overall FrankenLibC development process. Covers: baseline performance capture for all 250 symbols, evidence ledger extensions for safety decisions, property-based testing framework, CI artifact gates, and decision contracts for strict/hardened mode. These are the foundational capabilities that make all other epics possible — without baseline measurements, you cannot prove optimization. Without evidence ledger, you cannot audit safety decisions. Without property-based testing, you cannot find edge cases. Without CI gates, you cannot prevent regressions.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Methodology and infrastructure foundations from CMM/CMMI Level 3+ practices. Measurement-driven development from Basili & Weiss (1984) GQM paradigm.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- Baseline infrastructure: criterion.rs benchmark suite in benches/.
- Evidence ledger: src/evidence/ledger.rs with append-only storage.
- Property-based testing: proptest strategies in tests/properties/.
- CI gates: .github/workflows/gates.yml orchestrating all quality checks.
- Decision contract: src/tsm/decision_contract.rs as const state machine.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
1. Baseline capture operational for all 250 symbols.
2. Evidence ledger extended with galaxy-brain card format.
3. Property-based tests covering all 6 ABI families.
4. CI artifact gates enforced on all PRs.
5. Decision contract implemented and verified.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Each subtask produces structured JSON artifacts for CI consumption and historical tracking.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:39.572460211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:38.233349439Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:38.233139201Z","close_reason":"Delivered or tracked as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":977,"issue_id":"bd-3h1u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3h1u.1","title":"Capture baseline p50/p95/p99 for all 250 implemented symbols","description":"Capture comprehensive baseline performance measurements for all 250 currently implemented symbols. For each symbol: (1) Design representative input vectors (small/medium/large inputs, aligned/unaligned for memory ops, short/long strings for string ops). (2) Measure with criterion.rs: p50, p95, p99 latency in nanoseconds. (3) Measure in 3 configurations: raw (no TSM), strict mode, hardened mode. (4) Record: CPU cycles (rdtsc), wall clock (criterion), instructions retired (perf stat), cache misses (perf stat). (5) Store as structured JSON artifacts with: symbol name, ABI family, input description, measurement timestamp, system info (CPU model, frequency, kernel version). (6) Generate summary report: per-family latency distribution, hotspot identification (symbols where strict overhead > 20ns or hardened overhead > 200ns), optimization opportunity ranking. This baseline is the foundation for all future optimization work — you cannot improve what you do not measure. CI integration: baseline must be re-captured on each release candidate and compared against previous release.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Performance measurement methodology from Mytkowicz et al. (2009). Criterion.rs statistical framework. Benchmark design from Curtsinger & Berger (2013) Stabilizer.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- SymbolBenchSuite: macro that generates criterion benchmarks for all 250 symbols.
- Three configs: raw (no TSM), strict, hardened. Each measured independently.
- System info collection: /proc/cpuinfo parsing, uname -r, lscpu for L1/L2/L3 cache sizes.
- Output: symbol_baselines.json with per-symbol, per-config p50/p95/p99 in nanoseconds.
- CI: cargo bench --bench baseline_capture -- --output-format=json, archived as release artifact.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 250 symbols benchmarked in all 3 configurations (750 benchmark points).
2. Each benchmark statistically significant: coefficient of variation <5% across 3 runs.
3. Hotspot identification: symbols with strict_overhead > 20ns flagged in report.
4. Per-family summary: average overhead for string, stdlib, math, stdio, thread families.
5. Baseline stored as versioned JSON artifact with system fingerprint.
6. CI comparison: new baseline compared against previous, regressions >10% flagged.
7. Benchmark completes in <30 minutes on CI runner (parallelized across symbol families).","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Per-symbol: JSON record with symbol, family, config, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, iterations, measurement_time_s.
- Summary: baseline_summary.json with family averages, hotspot list, system info.
- CI diff: baseline_comparison.json with symbol, previous, current, delta_pct, verdict.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyBass","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:50.183556790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:01.743511305Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:01.743286319Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3h1u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":978,"issue_id":"bd-3h1u.1","author":"ScarletWaterfall","text":"Progress update (bd-3h1u.1): implemented deterministic symbol-level baseline inventory scaffolding for all 250 exported symbols.

Artifact currently records explicit measured-vs-pending coverage for raw/strict/hardened p50/p95/p99 per symbol (no fabricated values), fixture-backed capture readiness, and a deterministic priority queue for capture waves.

Validation run:
